text: Tailoring and fashion design is a thriving business for youth, especially in urban and peri-urban Uganda. Startup requires: a sewing machine (second-hand Singer: UGX 300,000-500,000, new industrial: UGX 800,000-1,500,000), fabrics for samples, measuring tape, scissors, threads, buttons, and a small workspace. Total startup: UGX 500,000-2,000,000 depending on equipment quality. Revenue sources: custom clothing (dresses UGX 30,000-100,000+, shirts UGX 20,000-50,000), alterations (UGX 5,000-15,000 each), and school uniforms in bulk. A skilled tailor in Kampala can earn UGX 500,000-1,500,000/month.

School uniform contracts are the golden opportunity for tailoring businesses. Schools order 100-500+ uniforms before each term (3 times/year). A single uniform (shirt + skirt/trouser) can be made for UGX 15,000-20,000 in materials and sold for UGX 35,000-50,000. Profit per uniform: UGX 15,000-30,000. Strategy: visit school head teachers in January/February before Term 1 rush. Offer sample uniforms for free. Provide bulk discounts. Deliver on time — reliability is more important than price.

Marketing for tailoring businesses: 1) Instagram and Facebook — post before/after photos of your work. 2) Dress well yourself — you are your best advertisement. 3) Offer free alterations to first 5 customers to build portfolio. 4) Partner with fabric shops — they refer customers to you, you refer customers to them. 5) Church/mosque events — offer to make matching outfits for groups at a discount. 6) Graduation season (November-December) is peak — advertise early. 7) Create a simple catalogue on your phone to show potential customers.
